tokens: Shorty is clearly a darker-rimmed crater.
The inner wall is quite blocky but...Except for the western portion of it, which is less blocky than the others.
The floor is hummocky, as we thought it was in the (Apollo 15) photographs.
The central peak, if you will, or central mound, is very blocky and jagged.
And the impression I have of the other mounds in the bottom is that they look like slump masses that may have come off the side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's just what they look like.
I'm not sure that...They have a bench appearance.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and the number 1 and 2 priorities at this station will be samples from the crater rim and the pan from the crater rim.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we've got a large boulder of very intensely fractured rock, right on the rim, right near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looks like a finely vesicular version of our clinopyroxene gabbro.
It's obviously crystalline and has generally that same appearance.
There is, in one spot here, some inclusions of a darker gray rock also intensely fractured.
The fracture systems, I think, will show up well in the flightline stereo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We have TV, and you might brush the (TV) lens for us before you run away.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I've got to get my battery covers cleaned.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm going to take a pan while I'm waiting for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we're going to want the SEP opened and dusted as well here.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, don't move it until I see it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't move it until I see it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I stirred it up with my feet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can see it from here!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wait a minute, let me put my visor up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got to dig a trench, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I guess we'd better work fast.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, he's not going out of his wits.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Is it the same color as cheese?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It's almost the same color as the LMP decal on my camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking toward the orange soil near the boulder) That is orange, Jack!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, this brush is getting harder to get on and off, too.
But I sure don't want to lose it.
Man, I may start putting that under my seat.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, zap me with a little cold water.
Okay, the SEP has been dusted.
I think I gave you 102 or something like that (for the SEP receiver temperature).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can see this in your color television, I'll bet you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How can there be orange soil on the Moon?!
Tell Ron (Evans) to get the lunar sounder over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks just like an oxidized desert soil, that's exactly right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'm going to clean their glasses so they know we're (not crazy)...(To Fendell) Can you wait a minute on that pan you're taking?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, I mean the television camera.
(To Fendell) I'll put you back where I had you.
(Pause) Now, I'll put you right (back) where you finished your pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You know (pause) that orange is along a line, Geno, along the rim crest.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, if there ever was a (chuckles)...I'm not going to say it.
(And then does, anyway) But if there ever was something that looked like a fumarole alteration, this is it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Bob) Okay, let me give you a gravimeter.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to go to Standby.
I got to get my gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, I think we hit one of those things we've got to reconsider on, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The problem is we're looking at PLSS constraints right now, as luck would have it, of course.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It seems to have died slowly there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Now, I'm going to give you another Standby and another Mark.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, I've trenched across the trend of the yellow...(correcting himself) or the orange.
There is light gray material on either side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Gene, we're going to have to...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You need to get a down-Sun color...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We also got to get that rock up there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let's start sampling that trench.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Look at where the contact between the gray and the...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Right, and it's on both sides (of the circumferential band)...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Before you disturb it, let me just get a couple of close-ups of that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, can you get a down-Sun?
I think your color will be best down-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get a little closer, Geno, if you think you're minimum.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, you want any of this bagged in the can, Bob?
Canned in the bag...or whatever it is?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's get the short can for some of that and...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, the color is...(Listens to Bob) Okay, let us get the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Better sample it first, then we'll get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Aah...Go back and get that one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll go get a new chunk.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me that, and get a new one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to slow down here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see when your shadow is there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you get around on the other side?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Because I can't see to sample.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See if you can get a sample right across that contact too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 509 has got the orange material from, oh, about 2 to 3 inches down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the light gray, which is on either side...We sampled the...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Want me to get some more?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: All of this is getting mixed a little bit with about a half-centimeter thick light-gray or medium-gray covering over the whole area.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, the gray material that's adjacent to the red material is in - what would I say - (bag) 510.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I had it, and I can't see it now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And that orange band is about a meter wide, I think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can't get to the bottom of it though, can you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I haven't been able to yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: [Moving to the north end of the trench] Just to be sure, why don't we sample this side of it, too.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Then I'm going to go get the can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If I can remember where we put it.
Bob, where did we put the small can?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's in bag 7 under my seat.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Commenting on the amount of soil Jack has poured in the sample bag) That's good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 511 has the gray from the other side of the orange band.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the other side happens to be the crater side.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you look around a minute, and I'll get that can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to see if this goes on down here as a zone.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It looks like it's an ellipsoidal area if my footprints are any indication.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to get the double core here instead of the small can.
Double core, please, instead of the small can.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Did you want it in the orange?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We can put cores in gray soil all the time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Do you want to go sideways a little with it?
(Pause) Or you just want to get it as deep as you can, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I expect we want to get...Let's go as deep as we can in the orange, please, there, Jack.
And the one problem at this station, Jack, is not that...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Responding to Bob's "deep as we can") All right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...we can decide priorities between this station or any other station.
It's the fact that we're running up against the walkback constraints here in just a very few minutes, about two-zero (20) minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, and the bottom (core) will be 44, and the top will be 35.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And after the core, we'd like for you to go over and sample some of the big rocks there on the rim, if you could very quickly.
That'll be the next order of priority after that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And I'm not sure whether your pan will look down into the crater or not, Jack.
But if it didn't, we'd like to get another one from there.
Hey, there's the crater (on the TV).
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, look into it yourself and then, I'll also get you a stereo pan before we leave.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've practiced too long on taking stereo pans of craters, without getting one here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got mine from right down there, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, it's a piece of glass, probably.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, how about right up here, (just west of the trench, for the core)?
(Pause) You know, we just about got [laughs] to the upper edge of this little ellipsoid zone.
I think we're going to have to...We've messed up most of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got a little piece of glass in my pocket.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, the upper portion of the core is going to be a little bit disturbed, because we've walked around the area so much.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That was a little piece of black glass...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Gene) You may want to get uphill...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That...Did you get a...Hold it, and I'll get a shot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's about as far as I could shove it in (by hand).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17, while you're doing that, was the gray mantle over the top of this, or was this showing all the way through to the surface?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, it was over the top.
About a half a centimeter over the top.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: He's getting about 3 centimeters a whack.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll tell you, it's a lot harder going in than that double core was back there (at Station 3).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) It acts like it's inherently cohesive.
It breaks up in angular fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The central portion of the zone actually has a crimson hue, or a red hue.
And outside of that, it's gray.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's all right, take it easy.
I'd offer to hit it, but I don't think I can, my hands are so tired.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let me hit some more.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you ...I'm afraid ...Jack, it's stable enough.
Why don't you get out of the way?
I'm afraid if I leave go of this thing, you'll get it in the head.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: He's still getting a centimeter a whack, poor guy.
I didn't get a locator, I better get a locator.
(Pause) Oh, it's in the (pan)...No, it isn't.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The only thing I question is our ability to get it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Breathing heavily) Man, that's really hit bottom.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, do I have core tubes on me now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...I really wonder about getting it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, we'll give it the old college try.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, we ought to be good at getting cores out by now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It wouldn't dare not come out.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is there enough to hold on to?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I was just getting this out for you.
I can get this side better.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Slowly so I can cap it all right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Even the (outside of the) core tube is red!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The bottom one's black and orange, and the top one's gray and orange!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The fact is, the bottom of the core is very black compared to anything we've seen.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, we must have gone through the red soil because it's filled, but it's filled with a black material.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Dark gray, almost a very, very fine grained...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Capping the bottom of the core) But it, it, it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me...God, it is black isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got to get it so I can get the...Boy, it is black and is it contrasted to that orange stuff.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, turn that thing so I can push this cap a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's just easier to turn the tube than my hand...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't want this cap to come off.
Now you don't have any caps, so let's take this back to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you take a picture of the hole, while you've got a camera there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Standing south of the core hole) Well, the hole's mostly in shadow.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Responding to Bob's confirmation about the caps) Yeah, I got them Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...And, 17, for your thought...We have to be leaving here...
We have to be leaving here in fourteen minutes.
On the move, because of walkback constraints.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we'd like to get a quick sample of the basalt up there on the rim, and Gene's stereo pan, and then press on.
And I emphasize that it's walkback constraint we're up against in 14 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'll sample it by hand.
And I'll get it in a bag in a minute since I don't have any.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Come back this way when you do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I better come there first, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I don't need it right this second...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you leave the core there Gene, and you can take your stereo pan while Jack's getting that sample.
And then you can get together and ram the core home.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (At the Rover, disassembling the core) Okay.
Bob, the bottom of the upper core is also dark.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Sounds (like the orange layer is) a little thin.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, like you might expect, the top of the bottom core is dark, too!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Garbled; pause) If I ever saw a classic alteration halo around a volcanic crater, this is it.
It's ellipsoidal; it appears to be zoned.
There's one sample we didn't get.
We didn't get the more yellowy stuff, we got the center portion...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get those caps, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What's holding...That's what's holding it, holding it from coming out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, the basalt (from the large boulder) is in bag 512.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm getting in your bag (SCB) here, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, our lock is on the outside here, so we ought to watch this gate.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Not going to worry about it.
This (gate) lock is on the outside of that lever lock.
You'll see what I mean when you look at the lock.
(Pause) Okay, I'm going to go get my pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The cores are not rammed yet.
You want to ram them while you're here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What did you do with my extension handle?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And if you want to ram them (probably handing Jack the rammer)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm going several meters around to the east and (slightly) towards the south to get this pan.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm circum...I'm on the circum...Oh, you know, on the rim.
Oh, that ought to be a beautiful shot, if I could see what my settings are.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, the lower core is "chucky-jam" full.
(A favorite expression of one of Schmitt's college professors, R.
Jahns.) I don't think I've budged that thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and, Jack, I copied - aside from three trench samples - I copied one single bag of basalt samples.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Looking west) Hey, Bob, from where I am, (I can look at a place) about 100 meters around the west side of the rim of this crater (where) the mantle on the inside of the rim turns from this gray material we've been sampling, in here, to a very dark gray material.
And there's a lot of orange stuff that goes down - radially down - into the pit of the crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Bob, those cores didn't feel like the follower went down at all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Shouldn't it have gone a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Not necessarily, if it's pretty compact stuff.
You were having a hard time getting it in.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I thought there was a little space up there, but maybe I just didn't feel it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think there's much danger of them coming apart.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got to take a couple of more pictures at that contact slope over there.
You can't see it from where you are, Jack, but I guess we got to leave.
Otherwise it would be nice to sample that dark stuff up on top.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need you guys rolling in 7 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We can get a...Oh, I bet I'm out of film!
Well, I got them all anyway, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Gesturing toward the west) That stuff (inside the crater rim) - and you're looking at me with the camera - that stuff is up toward that boulder.
About as far away from that boulder on the other side as we are on this side.
And we want a hack at that boulder, too.
Jack, let's see if we can't get that boulder, anyway.
(Pause) But I don't have any film.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Guys, we don't have that much time.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) There's a lot of little pieces - not a lot - but enough that I've seen five or six of them.
I got one in my pocket.
(Pause) This boulder that you were looking at with the TV, I'm going to take a sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm sorry, I didn't know you got that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 461 has another sample of basalt that I picked up right near where we dug the trench.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Bob, I'm going to give you something with the TV.
I want to show you where that dark material starts.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Putting a sample in Gene's SCB) Hold still, now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As you look at the inner rim - as it goes down (cough) to the right - you see a lot of boulders, a lot of rocks - that are protruding out.

Where that rock pattern thins out, just beyond that is an orange - a visible orange - radial pattern, and then beyond that is a definite change in albedo where you get the gray material, and a definite change in the number of rocks on the slope.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...and that particular material...(Hearing Bob again) That par...(Stopping a last time for Bob) Let me finish, Bob!
That particular rim material there continues around to the due north, and then there's a drastic change again where you see the inner rim completely terraced with this boulder fill.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I can't bet on it, but I can see it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...and you can talk about it when you get home.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: As long as you're happy, I am.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got to get the film changed, Jack...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All right, Gene, change the film at the next...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We can save time that way.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And we would like the SEP turned on before you leave...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: ...and we'd like EP number 1 taken, Jack, so you can deploy that at Victory.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll get your scoop for you.
Have you been leaving it cocked like that?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Of course, we haven't been taking any SEP measurements, and I don't know what difference it makes.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess I owe you a reading, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've been reading you loud and...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, we copy that, Geno, and...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Charge number 1 and we need the SEP, On...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We got the gnomon, we got the rake...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get charge number 1 for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll just hand it (the charge) to you (after Jack is seated).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And what's your frame count, Jack?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, (SEP) power's On, recorder's On, the temperature is one-twelve (112).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you get it (the charge), Geno?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) You get in (and) I'll hand it to you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And then I'm going to get the TV.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Jack, what's your frame count, please?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Wait, Bob, I can give you that on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I thought you were on there.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, camera Mode switch is 1; (TV) camera's going Aft.
Well, I guess that's the breaks of life.
Low-gain I'll set when I get on.
I'll give you (Jack) this (charge) when you're ready.
First thing I got to do, Bob, is change film at the next station.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Bob, LMP is at (frame count) seven-five
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say again, there, Jack, I missed that.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I must be getting fatter, you know it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Depends on how you get in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, we got a (warning) flag on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We got a flag on the Rover, and I'm reading 136 on battery number 2.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Say again on that one, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm reading 136 - make that 132 - on battery number 2, and we did get a flag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
